comparisonProtocol.phaseOrder: Runtime group visible starts should follow the reference group order and stay within roughly 0.5s of each object-track window unless a candidate explicitly widens a score window.
comparisonProtocol.pathFamily: Use canonicalComparisonPathFamily for candidate gates. Preserve semanticPathFamily and objectTrackExecutionFamily as inspectable source context so legacy label drift remains visible.
comparisonProtocol.objectTrackFit: Compare each runtime group against both aggregate group object-track targets and the selected primary object track.
comparisonProtocol.lowerFieldOverstay: Flag positive lower-field deltas above 0.18, and separately flag under-field deltas below -0.25 when the target expects a lower pass.
comparisonProtocol.spacing: Use npm run harness:check:challenge-motion-profile for spacing and bunching risk after any runtime candidate.
comparisonProtocol.scoreableWindows: Use shot-opportunity probes and group scoreWindowRead values; promotion requires clear multi-target lanes and no center-lane-only collapse.
comparisonProtocol.safety: No enemy shots, no enemy attack starts, and no ship losses remain hard blockers.

runtimePromotionBlockers: Stage 7 target-video object-track fit is 4.7/10, below the first promotion floor of 5.0/10.

Group 1: raise aggregate object-track fit to first promotion floor; raise primary object-track fit to first promotion floor; reduce aggregate path-length ratio below first promotion floor; reduce primary path-length ratio below first promotion floor.

Group 2: legacy semantic label differs from canonical object-track execution family; runtime follows canonical object-track family rather than legacy semantic label; raise aggregate object-track fit to first promotion floor; raise primary object-track fit to first promotion floor; reduce lower-field overstay against aggregate target; reduce aggregate path-length ratio below first promotion floor; reduce primary path-length ratio below first promotion floor.
Group 3: reduce aggregate path-length ratio below first promotion floor.

Group 4: legacy semantic label differs from canonical object-track execution family; runtime follows canonical object-track family rather than legacy semantic label; raise primary object-track fit to first promotion floor; avoid lower-field undershoot against aggregate target; reduce aggregate path-length ratio below first promotion floor; reduce primary path-length ratio below first promotion floor.

Group 5: legacy semantic label differs from canonical object-track execution family; runtime follows canonical object-track family rather than legacy semantic label; raise aggregate object-track fit to first promotion floor; raise primary object-track fit to first promotion floor; reduce aggregate path-length ratio below first promotion floor.
pathFamilyResolutions: Group 2: canonical hook-arc from object-track-and-motion-spec; legacy semantic label remains cross-sweep.
Group 4: canonical cross-sweep from object-track-and-motion-spec; legacy semantic label remains hook-arc.
Group 5: canonical hook-arc from object-track-and-motion-spec; legacy semantic label remains boss-led-loop.
primaryTrackGateResolutions: Group 1: primary track target-track-10 is the candidate tuning gate; aggregate track remains a guardrail.
Group 4: primary track target-track-110 is the candidate tuning gate; aggregate track remains a guardrail.
Group 5: primary track target-track-142 is the candidate tuning gate; aggregate track remains a guardrail.
summary.read: The Stage 7 execution description is precise enough for one measured runtime candidate; the current runtime is still not promotable without a player-visible improvement.

nextBestStep: Run one timing-aware Stage 7 candidate against this description, focused on groups 1, 4, and 5 path length/lower-field/late-loop shape, then produce before/after evidence and guardrail results.
